stones.title: Children of Thomas and Elinor Davies
stones.notes: In memory of the children of Thomas and Elinor Davies. Catharine [name partly worn] died April 27, 1852, aged 1 year, 2 months and 5 weeks. Mary died January 26, 1860, aged 1 month. Richard died November 26, 1863, aged 6 months and 2 weeks.
stones.trans:
ER COF AM
Plant Thomas ac Elinor Davies.
Catharine [?] a fu farw Ebrill 27, 1852,
yn 1 flwydd, 2 fis, a 5 wythnos oed.
Mary a fu farw Ion. 26, 1860. Yn 1
mis oed.
Richard a fu farw Tach. 26, 1863.
Yn 6 mis, 2 wythnos oed.

stones.title: William Roberts
stones.trans:
IN
MEMORY
OF
WILLIAM ROBERTS,
SON OF
JOHN & ELENOR ROBERTS,
WHO DEPARTED THIS LIFE
JULY 8th 1865,
AGED 27 YEARS & 8 MONTHS.

stones.title: William Lloyd
stones.notes:
Englyn

In a snug cottage he was born,
In California's land;
In Havre de Grace he died,
Soon after reaching shore;
And here he lies at rest,
Quiet in the grave,
Until the morning he is raised
To live in the land of peace.
stones.trans:
ER COF
Am
William mab Robert ac Eleanor Lloyd,
yr hwn a fu farw Gorphenaf 17 A.D. 1859,
yn 1 flwydd, 9 mis a 14 diwrnod oed.

Mewn bwthyn clyd fe'i ganwyd,
Yn Nghalifornia dir;
Yn Havre de Grace bu farw,
Yn ebrwydd 'rol cyrhaedd tir;
Ac yma mae yn gorphwys,
Yn dawel yn y bedd,
Hyd foreu fe'i hadferir
I fyw i wlad yr hedd.

stones.title: Owen K. Thomas
stones.notes:
Englyn

My beloved spouse sleeps here;
I shall seek him no more,
Alas, he will not return,
His strength spent in a bed of earth.
stones.trans:
ER COF AM
OWEN K. THOMAS.
Gynt o Bethesda, Arfon.
Bu farw Medi 8, 1883.
Oed 56 mlwydd.

Fy mhriod hynod sy'n huno, yma
Wnir mwyach ei geisio;
Ataf ni ddychwel eto,
Gwael ei rym mewn gwely o ro.

ER COF AM
CATHRINE THOMAS
Ganwyd Awyst 1826
Bu farw Chwefrol 1918

byrhaodd fy nyddiau.

stones.title: Stevens
stones.notes: Modern pictorial slate: sandblasted treeline, cabin, and canoeist, with a full-moon piercing cut through the stone — a contemporary continuation of the Monson slate tradition (a small plaque reads Bob Stevens).
stones.trans:
STEVENS

Here on these beloved hills
that he loved so much
his life has come full circle
His roots run deep on this spot
In Monson, Maine

Husband, Father, Grandfather
and Best Friend

stones.title: Ebenezer Grosvenor
stones.trans:
To the memory of the
Rev. Ebenezer Grosvenor
late pastor of the congregational church
in Harvard,
descended from respectable parents
in Pomfret, Connecticut,
educated at Yale College
in New Haven,
of such endowments as rendered him
an ornament & blessing
in the various relations which he sustained.
He was a good steward in the house of God,
and discharged the duties of his pastoral office
with prudence & impartiality, care & fidelity;
he was a man of polite address
and peculiarly formed for social life,
a tender & loving husband,
an affectionate & kind parent,
an agreeable friend & pleasing companion;
he was much beloved & respected in life,
in death greatly lamented,
and is, we trust, receiving the reward of his
labours in the kingdom of his Lord.
His bereaved & grateful people have erected
this stone the monument of his virtues,
& their affection.
He was the beloved pastor of the first church
in Scituate 17 years
And in Harvard 6.
He died May 28, 1788
Aged 49.

Blessed are the dead that die in the Lord,
for they rest from their labours & their works
do follow them.

stones.title: Jonathan Sawyer
stones.trans:
Erected
In Memory of
Mr. Jonathan Sawyer,
who died Sept. 9, 1815.
Æt. 42.

And must this body die,
This well-wrought frame decay?
And must these active limbs of mine
Lie mouldering in the clay?

Corruption, earth, and worms,
Shall but refine this flesh,
Till my triumphant spirit comes
To put it on afresh.

Christ my redeemer lives,
And ever from the skies
Looks down and watches all my dust
Till he shall bid it rise.

stones.trans:

Daughter of Mr.

ASENATH POLLARD,

My days were few, and I but small,
But must obey when Christ doth call.

stones.title: Farwell Family
stones.trans:
In memory of
Capt.
JOHN FARWELL,
who died
Aug. 8, 1824.
Æt. 46

In memory of
Mrs. ARATHUSA
wife of
Capt. John Farwell,
who died
July 23, 1886.
Æt. 52.

Forgive, blest shade, the tributary tear,
That mourns your exit from a world like this;
Forgive the wish that would have kept thee here,
And stayed thy progress to the seats of bliss.

No more confined to grovelling scenes of night,
No more are tenants pent in mortal clay,
Why should we rather hail your glorious flight,
And trace your journey to the realms of day.

stones.title: Martha Green
stones.notes: Slate source was Pin Hill Quarry, Carver was the Park Family from Groton, MA
stones.trans:
In memory of Mrs Martha
Green (& Infant Babe) the
Amiable & virtuous Consort
of Doct Peter Green who
departed this Life June 8th
1770. In the 25th year aetat.

To the deep Regret of those who
were so happy as to be nearly
allied to her and the Great Grief
of numerous Friends whose
Esteem her affable & obliging
Deportment had justly
merited.

[remaining lines worn, illegible]

stones.title: John Safford
stones.trans:
Memento mori

Erected to the memory
of Mr John Safford,
who departed this Life
June 2d 1782. In the
74th year of his Age.

Crush'd as a moth beneath thy hands
We moulder to the dust,
Our feeble pow'rs can ne'er withstand
And all our beauty's lost.

This mortal life decays apace,
How soon the bubble's broke,
Adam and all his numerous Race
Are [...] [illegible].

stones.title: Chandler Robbins
stones.trans:
This Stone
Consecrated to the memory
Of the Revd Chandler Robbins, D.D.
was erected
By the inhabitants of the first
Religious Society in Plymouth
As their last grateful tribute of respect
For his eminent labors
In the ministry
Of Jesus Christ
Which commenced January 30 1760
And continued till his death June 30 1799
Wh[en] Ætatis 61
When he entered into the everlasting rest
Prepared for the faithful embassadors
Of the most high God

Ah come heav'n's radiant Offspring hither throng
Behold your prophet your Elijah fled
Let sacred symphony attune each tongue
To chant [...] with the virtuous dead.

THOMAS
CUSHMAN

stones.title: Thomas Cushman
stones.trans:
HERE LYETH BURIED Y[e]
OF THAT PRECIOUS SERVANT
OF GOD M[r] THOMAS CUSHMAN, W[HO]
AFTER HE HAD SERVED HIS
GENERATION, ACCORDING TO
THE WILL OF GOD, AND
PARTICULARLY THE CHURCH OF
PLYMOUTH FOR MANY YEARS, IN
THE OFFICE OF A RULING ELDER
FELL ASLEEP IN JESUS DECEM[BER]
Y[e] 10, 169[1], & IN Y[e]
84 YEAR OF HIS AGE.

This Stone, placed at the grave of
Elder Cushman, by the First Church in
Plymouth, was removed to this situa-
tion in 1858, to make room for a more
enduring memorial, which now exactly
occupies its original position.

stones.title: John Howland
stones.notes: Memorial to Mayflower passenger John Howland (d. 1672/3, double-dated) with a carved ship in the tympanum; a later commemorative stone (the 1958 Howland Society marker stands behind).
stones.trans:
Here ended the Pilgrimage of
JOHN HOWLAND
who died February 23, 167 2/3.
aged above 80 years.
He married Elizabeth daughter of
JOHN TILLEY
who came with him in the
Mayflower Dec. 1620.
From them are descended a
numerous posterity.

"Hee was a godly man and an ancient
professor in the wayes of Christ. Hee was
one of the first comers into this land and
was the last man that was left of those
that came over in the Shipp called the
Mayflower that lived in Plymouth."
Plymouth Records

stones.title: Abraham Hammatt
stones.notes: Father and daughter dead of the same fever within two months — likely the 1797 yellow-fever season. The closing couplet is unusually literary; long-s used throughout (sacred = 'facred').
stones.trans:
In
This sacred spot
Are deposited the remains
of Capt Abraham Hammatt
Who died of a malignant
Fever October 13th 1797 AEtatis 47
And of his daughter Sophia who
On the 1st December following
Fell a victim to the same
Disease AEtatis 13 [?]

Hers was the mildness of the rising Morn
And his the radiance of the risen day

stones.title: Lothrop Barlett
stones.notes: Six-week-old infant; medallion-and-vine borders, winged skull. Final date line partly lost at the ground line.
stones.trans:

OF LOTHROP BARTLETT
SON TO SAMUEL
& ELIZth BARTLETT
AGED 6 WEEKS
DECd SEPtr Ye 2[?]
17[3?][?]

stones.notes: Bold skull-and-crossbones tympanum with small winged faces in the upper corners.
stones.trans:
Here lies Interr'd the
Body of Mr
JOHN BARTLETT
who departed this
life February the 6th
AD 1773 AE 77 Years

stones.notes: 1681 — one of the oldest surviving stones on Burial Hill; the original slate tablet is preserved set into a later granite surround. Note V for U throughout (ABOVT, IVNE).
stones.trans:
HERE LYETH Ye BODY
OF EDWARD GRAY
GENT AGED ABOVT
52 YEARS & DEPARTD
THIS LIFE Ye LAST OF
IVNE 1681

stones.notes: Soul effigy with distinctive side-curled hair; the age line runs at the ground line.
stones.trans:
In Memory of
Mrs SARAH BARTLETT
wife of
Dean JOSEPH BARTLETT
who died Decr 23. 1785
in ye 80 year of her age

stones.title: David Croswell
stones.trans:
In Memory of
David son of Mr
Joseph Croswell &
Mrs Lucy his wife,
who died Sepr 29th
1783 aged 1 year 2
months & 8 days.

stones.title: Sarah Morton
stones.notes: A 22-year-old wife; closing couplet partly obscured at the ground line.
stones.trans:
Here lyes buried
the Body of
Mrs SARAH MORTON
wife of
Mr LEMUEL MORTON
who departed this Life
April 17th 1785 in ye 23d
year of her Age.

In Her, United all that's Fair & good,
Short [...] her Race, yet Virtue's Path she trod

stones.title: Joanna Atwood
stones.notes: Winged skull tympanum with foliate scrollwork borders; lightly ruled guide lines still visible across the face.
stones.trans:
Here lyes the Body
of Mrs Joanna Atwood
ye wife to Mr JOHN
Atwood who decd Octo ye
29th 1762 IN ye 46th
year of her age
